element_name: Speaking appropriately for a variety of purposes and demonstrating attentiveness as a listener
outcome_text: Listen to obtain information relating to more than one option, e.g. listen to school related announcements, using a speaking timetable to get a train arrival and departure time

element_name: Speaking appropriately for a variety of purposes and demonstrating attentiveness as a listener
outcome_text: Ask questions to obtain information, e.g. to check dates/prices (face to face and by telephone), booking a meal over the telephone

element_name: Speaking appropriately for a variety of purposes and demonstrating attentiveness as a listener
outcome_text: Follow a series of spoken instructions under supervision, e.g. go to teacher's room, local shop, or post office, top up a mobile telephone

element_name: Speaking appropriately for a variety of purposes and demonstrating attentiveness as a listener
outcome_text: Express personal opinions, facts and feelings appropriately, e.g. expressing an opinion on a television programme, relate news from their weekend

element_name: Speaking appropriately for a variety of purposes and demonstrating attentiveness as a listener
outcome_text: Participate in practical, formal and informal communications, e.g. an interview or a parent teacher meeting, an interview with peers on interest related topics, chatting while out with friends, making announcements on the school intercom

element_name: Speaking appropriately for a variety of purposes and demonstrating attentiveness as a listener
outcome_text: Listen to and respond to a range of stories

element_name: Using non-verbal behaviour to get the message across
outcome_text: Identify a range of non-verbal communications methods, e.g. facial expression, tones of voice, symbols, clothing, colours to signal mood/appropriate action

element_name: Using non-verbal behaviour to get the message across
outcome_text: Use appropriate non-verbal behaviour in communicating a simple idea, e.g. disappointment or joy, tone of voice to seek assistance/complain

element_name: Using non-verbal behaviour to get the message across
outcome_text: Relay a response or request non-verbally, e.g. signalling a phone call

element_name: Using non-verbal behaviour to get the message across
outcome_text: Respond to non-verbal signals and signs encountered in daily life, e.g. road signs, traffic signs, hazardous materials

element_name: Using non-verbal behaviour to get the message across
outcome_text: Follow the sequence of non-verbal instructions or directions for a frequent activity, e.g. using household equipment with three or more operations, putting a battery in a toy, finding safety exits/following fire drill
Read familiar words that are commonly used and personally relevant, e.g. read a list of items relating to a personal interest/sport/hobby, names of family members
Use simple rules and text conventions that support meaning, e.g. pause at a full stop

Interpret different forms of writing and text, including social signs and symbols, e.g. common formats of bills, menus, forms, timetables, road and other signs, simple food preparation instructions (boil an egg, make a sandwich, make a cup of tea), short piece of personally relevant writing
Find key information from different forms of writing, e.g. locate factual information in forms/bills, times and dates of appointments, menus, timetables, newspapers
Use a range of reading strategies, e.g. clues, context, sound, prediction and decoding

element_name: Using a range of writing forms to express opinions
outcome_text: Write/type notes and messages needed for simple tasks, e.g. address an envelope

element_name: Using a range of writing forms to express opinions
outcome_text: Write/type at least five sentences so that they convey meaning or information, e.g. arrange a meeting with a friend, give directions

element_name: Using a range of writing forms to express opinions
outcome_text: Use the main rules of writing appropriately, e.g. use capitals and full stops

element_name: Using a range of writing forms to express opinions
outcome_text: Use a range of spelling patterns, e.g. add 'ing' to a word – drop, double or nothing

element_name: Using a range of writing forms to express opinions
outcome_text: Use a range of different forms of writing to suit purpose and audience, e.g. write a cheque, fill a simple form, complete a diary entry
Participate in a performance or a presentation, e.g. presentation of a short drama piece to members of the class, performance of dance or music to parents
Create a range of images using a variety of materials
Produce a piece of work for display
Listen to a range of music and respond by discussing thoughts and feelings, e.g. favourite singer and say why they like their music
Use drama or dance to explore real and imaginary situations

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Identify three everyday uses of technology, e.g. for learning, working, and for fun

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Use technology requiring not more than three functions, for personal, home and educational/workplace use, e.g. assistive technologies, mobile phone (pre-programmed numbers), photocopier, computer, camera, DVD/video player, household appliances

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Use technology to communicate in an activity with others

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Use a new piece of ICT equipment

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Turn a personal computer on and off safely, e.g. following the steps to shut down a computer

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Identify the information symbols on a desktop, e.g. internet explorer symbol

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Use frequently used keys appropriately

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Use a software package, involving opening a package, entering and manipulating text/image/data, save to file, print and exit safely, e.g. clipart, word document, electronic presentation

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Access a range of websites on the internet, e.g. scoilnet, websites of personal interest to the student

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Find information for a project on the web

element_name: Using suitable technologies for a range of purposes
outcome_text: Send and open an email
Recognise frequently used Euro notes and coins
Pay for an item correctly and count the change in a mock-up or real life shopping transaction
Explain a shopping receipt, in relation to what was bought, money tendered and correct change given
Understand a common household bill in relation to the service provided, how much being charged and how it can be paid for
Recognise the difference between using money to buy essential items and luxury items
Plan a personal budget for a week
Save a small amount of money each week to buy an item
Recognise numbers up to 100 in N, e.g. knowing how many zeros for tens, hundreds
Recognise place value in relation to units, tens and hundreds, e.g. knowing how many zeros for tens, hundreds
Add two digit whole numbers that total less than 100 in the context of an everyday situation
Subtract two digit whole numbers in the context of an everyday situation
Estimate quantities to the nearest value in broad terms, e.g. to the nearest quantity in 10s or 100s as appropriate
Use appropriate words to describe temperature, e.g. hot and cold
Identify instruments used for indicating and adjusting temperature, e.g. thermometer, marked oven dials
Relate temperatures to everyday situations, e.g. heating in a classroom
Locate appropriate temperatures on a cooker dial, e.g. gas mark 4, 200 degrees Celsius
Compare temperatures for the different times of the year, e.g. hot in summer and cold in winter, keep a simple weather log

element_name: Developing an awareness of weight and capacity
outcome_text: Use appropriate vocabulary to describe the units of weight and capacity, e.g. litres, 500ml, kilograms, grams (pictorial or concrete)

element_name: Developing an awareness of weight and capacity
outcome_text: Identify the marks for the units of weight and capacity, e.g. using a measuring jug, using a weighing scale

element_name: Developing an awareness of weight and capacity
outcome_text: List some examples of weight and capacity from daily life, e.g. knowing own weight, a litre of milk

element_name: Developing an awareness of weight and capacity
outcome_text: Use a graduated vessel to work out the capacity of liquids, e.g. using a jug to measure litre of milk

element_name: Developing an awareness of weight and capacity
outcome_text: Use a weighing scales to work out the weight of powders and solids, e.g. weighing the ingredients for a cake

element_name: Developing an awareness of length and distance
outcome_text: Use appropriate vocabulary to describe the units in length and distance, e.g. kilometres, metres, centimetres

element_name: Developing an awareness of length and distance
outcome_text: Identify the units of length and distance on a ruler, metre stick and measuring tape

element_name: Developing an awareness of length and distance
outcome_text: Use a ruler to draw and measure different lengths of lines

element_name: Developing an awareness of length and distance
outcome_text: Estimate the length of common objects, e.g. the length of a book

element_name: Developing an awareness of length and distance
outcome_text: Measure the length of common places, e.g. bedroom, kitchen, classroom using a measuring tape
Find digits 0-9 and the decimal point and necessary operations buttons (+, -, x, ÷, =) on a calculator
Use a calculator to solve simple problems, e.g. add two items
Use a calculator to correct work which has been completed without the use of a calculator
Find and use a calculator on a mobile phone to work out how much several items will cost in a shopping trip
Use appropriate vocabulary to describe 2D shapes, e.g. triangle, square, rectangle and circle
Identify 2D shapes in the environment, e.g. road signs
Use appropriate vocabulary to describe 3D shapes, e.g. cube, cylinder, cone and sphere
Identify 3D shapes in the environment, e.g. cereal box, can of food
Complete a simple pattern using shapes, e.g. using tiles to make a pattern
Use appropriate vocabulary to describe direction and position, e.g. forwards, backwards, beside, between

element_name: Using data for a range of different purposes
outcome_text: Collect information using a range of methods, e.g. survey, observation, interview

element_name: Using data for a range of different purposes
outcome_text: Record information using a range of methods, e.g. tally marks, pictograms, simple bar charts

element_name: Using data for a range of different purposes
outcome_text: Sort information into categories, e.g. sorting by colour, size, shape

element_name: Using data for a range of different purposes
outcome_text: Present information using a range of methods, e.g. pictograms, simple bar charts, tables

element_name: Using data for a range of different purposes
outcome_text: Read simple graphs, tables and charts, e.g. interpreting a weather chart

element_name: Using data for a range of different purposes
outcome_text: Use data to answer simple questions, e.g. what is the most popular?
Identify lines, angles and shapes in the environment, e.g. corner of a page, edge of a table
Compare and order shapes by size, e.g. smallest to largest
Combine shapes to make pictures or patterns, e.g. using tangrams
Identify symmetry in shapes and objects, e.g. folding paper to show line of symmetry
Recognise tessellation in the environment, e.g. floor tiles, brick walls
Tell the time on an analogue and digital clock in hours, half hours and quarter hours
Recognise the relationship between different units of time, e.g. seconds, minutes, hours, days, weeks, months, years
Read and interpret a simple timetable, e.g. school timetable, TV guide
Use a calendar to find dates and record events, e.g. birthdays, holidays
Estimate duration of familiar activities, e.g. how long it takes to walk to school
Use a timer or stopwatch to time activities, e.g. cooking, games
Develop and maintain a daily personal care routine, e.g. washing, brushing teeth, combing hair
Identify and use appropriate personal care products, e.g. soap, shampoo, deodorant
Select and wear appropriate clothes for different occasions and weather, e.g. school, sports, going out
Maintain personal belongings in good order, e.g. keeping clothes clean and tidy
Recognise the importance of personal hygiene for health and social interaction
Take responsibility for own laundry, e.g. sorting, washing, drying, folding
Make appropriate choices about personal grooming, e.g. haircuts, skincare
Identify the main food groups and their importance for health, e.g. proteins, carbohydrates, fats, vitamins
Plan and prepare a simple healthy meal, e.g. sandwich, salad, soup
Recognise the importance of a balanced diet, e.g. eating a variety of foods
Identify foods that should be eaten in moderation, e.g. sugary foods, fatty foods
Practice good food hygiene, e.g. washing hands, storing food safely
Read and interpret food labels, e.g. ingredients, use by dates, nutritional information
Identify the benefits of regular physical activity, e.g. fitness, mental health
Participate in physical activities on a regular basis, e.g. walking, swimming, sports
Recognise the importance of rest and sleep for health
Identify the effects of alcohol, tobacco and drugs on health
Make informed choices about alcohol, tobacco and drugs
Identify signs and symptoms of common illnesses, e.g. cold, flu, headache
Know when and how to seek medical help, e.g. visiting a doctor, calling emergency services
Recognise signs and causes of stress, e.g. feeling anxious, physical symptoms
Identify personal triggers for stress, e.g. exams, social situations
Use strategies to manage stress, e.g. relaxation techniques, exercise, talking to someone
Identify sources of support for managing stress, e.g. family, friends, counsellors
Develop a personal plan for managing stress
Practice mindfulness and relaxation techniques, e.g. breathing exercises, meditation
Identify potential hazards in the home and how to avoid them, e.g. fire, electrical, slips and falls
Know what to do in an emergency, e.g. fire drill, calling emergency services
Practice basic first aid, e.g. treating minor cuts and burns
Stay safe when using the internet, e.g. protecting personal information, recognising online dangers
Understand physical and emotional changes during puberty
Develop an understanding of relationships and boundaries, e.g. friendship, romantic relationships
Recognise the importance of consent and respect in relationships
Identify sources of support and information about relationships and sexuality
Develop strategies for dealing with peer pressure
Identify and name a range of emotions, e.g. happy, sad, angry, scared
Recognise emotions in self and others, e.g. through facial expressions, body language
Express emotions in appropriate ways, e.g. talking, writing, art
Develop strategies for managing difficult emotions, e.g. anger, sadness
Show empathy towards others, e.g. understanding how others feel
Identify choices and decisions that need to be made in daily life
Consider the consequences of different choices before making a decision
Make informed decisions based on available information
Take responsibility for personal decisions and their outcomes
Seek advice and support when making difficult decisions
Identify different types of relationships, e.g. family, friends, acquaintances
Recognise qualities of a good friend, e.g. trust, loyalty, kindness
Develop and maintain friendships, e.g. sharing, taking turns, showing interest
Communicate effectively in relationships, e.g. listening, expressing feelings
Show respect for others, e.g. different opinions, cultures, abilities
Work cooperatively with others, e.g. in group tasks, team activities
Recognise and respond appropriately to bullying behaviour
Identify causes and signs of conflict, e.g. disagreements, anger
Use strategies to avoid conflict, e.g. walking away, staying calm
Resolve conflict through discussion and compromise
Seek help from an adult when unable to resolve conflict
Reflect on how conflict was resolved and what could be done differently
Practice forgiveness and reconciliation after conflict
Identify local facilities and services, e.g. library, post office, shops, leisure centre
Use local facilities independently, e.g. borrowing books, posting letters, making purchases
Use public transport safely and independently, e.g. bus, train
Follow rules and expectations in public places, e.g. queuing, being quiet in library
Identify people who can provide help and advice, e.g. family, teachers, community workers
Know how to contact emergency services, e.g. 999, 112
Ask for help appropriately in different situations
Use information sources to find help and advice, e.g. internet, phone directory
Know when and how to make a complaint, e.g. about a product or service
Identify needs versus wants when making purchases
Compare prices and value when shopping, e.g. comparing products, looking for deals
Understand consumer rights and responsibilities, e.g. receipts, returns, guarantees
Recognise and resist persuasive advertising techniques
Make informed choices about purchases based on quality, price and need
Consider environmental impact when making consumer choices, e.g. recycling, reducing waste

element_name: Being able to set goals for learning
outcome_text: Identify personal strengths, skills and interests

element_name: Being able to set goals for learning
outcome_text: Set realistic short-term and long-term goals for learning

element_name: Being able to set goals for learning
outcome_text: Develop a plan to achieve learning goals, e.g. identifying steps, resources needed

element_name: Being able to set goals for learning
outcome_text: Review and adjust goals as needed
Identify different types of jobs and careers
Research jobs of interest, e.g. job descriptions, qualifications required
Understand the role of education and training in preparing for work
Identify sources of information about jobs and careers, e.g. career guidance, internet
Recognise the value of work experience in exploring career options
Understand the importance of employability skills, e.g. communication, teamwork, problem-solving

element_name: Preparing for a work related activity
outcome_text: Prepare a simple CV or personal profile

element_name: Preparing for a work related activity
outcome_text: Write a simple cover letter or application

element_name: Preparing for a work related activity
outcome_text: Prepare for an interview, e.g. appropriate dress, punctuality, questions to ask

element_name: Preparing for a work related activity
outcome_text: Participate in a practice interview, e.g. role play, mock interview

element_name: Preparing for a work related activity
outcome_text: Reflect on interview experience and identify areas for improvement

element_name: Preparing for a work related activity
outcome_text: Understand workplace expectations, e.g. punctuality, dress code, following instructions

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Identify potential hazards in the workplace

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Understand the importance of following health and safety rules

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Use personal protective equipment (PPE) appropriately

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Follow safety procedures when using equipment, e.g. tools, machinery

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Report hazards and accidents appropriately

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Know the location of emergency exits and safety equipment

element_name: Developing an awareness of health and safety using equipment
outcome_text: Maintain a clean and safe work area

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Follow instructions and complete tasks accurately

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Work independently and as part of a team

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Manage time effectively during work tasks

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Communicate effectively with colleagues and supervisors

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Accept and respond to feedback constructively

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Show initiative and problem-solving skills during work tasks

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Demonstrate reliability and punctuality

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Reflect on work experience and identify learning

element_name: Taking part in a work related activity
outcome_text: Plan for transition from school to further education, training or employment
